name: Triple Chocolat Cake Part 2
recipeIngredient: Whipped ganache filling (made with cream, chocolate, and sugar)
Remaining glaze mixture (consists of sugar, water, and optional flavorings)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the cake on a 9-inch cardboard circle.
2. Slice the cake into two even layers using a serrated knife.
3. Lightly moisten the bottom layer with half the syrup using a pastry brush.
4. Whip the chilled filling with a wire whisk or electric mixer until stiff peaks form. Be careful not to over-whip, as this can cause graininess.
5. Spread one-third of the whipped ganache on the bottom cake layer and top it with the top layer.
6. Brush the remaining syrup over the top of the cake.
7. Spread the rest of the filling over the top and sides of the cake, ensuring a smooth surface.
8. Chill the cake in the refrigerator for at least an hour before glazing.
9. Prepare the glaze by pouring a small amount over your fingernail. The consistency should allow you to see your nail's outline through it.
10. If needed, thin the glaze by gently heating it with simmering water.
11. Place the cake on a wire rack set on a baking sheet with rims.
12. Pour the glaze over the center of the cake and let it flow down the sides.
13. Cover any gaps on the sides by scraping up glaze from the baking sheet.
14. Once fully covered, gently tap the cake (including the backing sheet and rack) on the counter to release air bubbles.
15. Use a pin to pop any remaining bubbles.
16. Chill the cake in the refrigerator until ready to serve.
